title: GE Lighting CES Demo
description: A web based lighting demo to showcase GE Lighting's various automated lighting setups and controls at their internal convention.

title: Sony Pet Venomizer
description: A promotional app for the final Venom movie that leveraged Getty Images' custom trained ML model to convert pictures of peoples' pets into 'venomized' versions.

title: AWE Hot Ones! Panel
description: I had the fortune of doing a panel at AWE 2025 with the one-and-only Joe Gabriel from Niantic. Turns out it was a Hot Ones interview

title: Google Brainwash
description: My one and only shipped game... was a 100% voice controlled puzzle for the Google Home where you would have to guess the word tying the images together.  It is sadly no longer on the store, but there does exist video of it working.

title: Morgan Stanley PGA Tour Through Time
description: This project is much more representative of the work I often found myself doing in XR.  This experience was linked to a specific golf course and users could go around to different holes and have a volumetric Justin Rose show the history that happened where they were standing.  While each individual part was not too hard, combining volumetric video, location locked content, achievement tracking, and 9 holes of content running on mobile, was certainly a challenge.

title: Disney Avatar 2 Creature Creator
description: A fishtank app created as a part of Disney's Avatar 2 conservation campaign.  You create a fish, and then you can let it swim around in a larger aquarium app.  However, we had to tie all the data together with a database because the tank and creator were on Unity/Web.  I spent HOURS creating instance shaders to get the underwater scene running on phone.

title: Mercedes F1 Garage Tour
description: This experience was originally made for the F1 event in vegas to showcase the F1 Garage, and we added additional features like an interactive reflex-testing game similar to what the F1 drivers train on. I spent most of my time building said reflex game, and helping port the app over to the Quest platform.
